content.subtitle: Use
rows: To pass on what another person has told us
She told me that she **had been living** abroad for two years.

content.subtitle: Form: direct speech to reported speech
rows:
present simple
'I **love** playing the guitar,' said Mark.

past simple
Mark said he **loved** playing the guitar.

present continuous
'We **are studying** for the exam,' they said.

past continuous
They said they **were studying** for the exam.

present perfect simple
'I **have visited** Paris twice,' Laura said.

past perfect simple
Laura said she **had visited** Paris twice.

present perfect continuous
'I **have been working** all morning,' said Ben.

past perfect continuous
Ben said he **had been working** all morning.

past simple
'My sister **won** the competition,' Sam said.

past perfect simple
Sam said his sister **had won** the competition.

past continuous
'I **was listening** to music,' said Kevin.

past perfect continuous
Kevin said he **had been listening** to music.

will
'I **will** help you with your project,' Alice said.

would
Alice said she **would** help me with my project.

am/is/are going to
'They **are going to** open a new shop,' said Ana.

was/were going to
Ana said they **were going to** open a new shop.

can
'I **can** speak French,' David said.

could
David said he **could** speak French.

must / have to
'You **must** wear a helmet,' the coach said.

had to
The coach said we **had to** wear a helmet.

may
'I **may** travel to Japan next year,' said Paul.

might
Paul said he **might** travel to Japan the following year.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: Tense changes only happen when the reporting verb (say, tell, etc.) is used in the past tense.
Past perfect simple and past perfect continuous are usually left unchanged.
Would, should, could and might are also generally kept the same.
Reporting verbs such as apologise, deny, promise, refuse and suggest can also be used in reported speech.

sections.title: A: Change to reported speech
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in reported speech.

items.sentence: 'I want to build a new house,' Jill said.
items.answer: Jill said she wanted to build a new house.

items.sentence: 'We are making a dress,' they said.
items.answer: They said they were making a dress.

items.sentence: 'I can draw quite well,' Emma said.
items.answer: Emma said she could draw quite well.

items.sentence: 'They are going to make a new one,' Joe said.
items.answer: Joe said they were going to make a new one.

items.sentence: 'I may visit the White House next week,' Polly said.
items.answer: Polly said she might visit the White House the following week.

items.sentence: 'I was writing a poem,' Tina said.
items.answer: Tina said she had been writing a poem.

sections.title: B: Complete the reported sentences
sections.instructions: Use the correct reported form of the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: Tom said he ...... (need) help with his homework.
items.answer: needed

items.sentence: My parents said they ...... (watch) TV at that moment.
items.answer: were watching

items.sentence: Sara said she ...... (finish) the project the day before.
items.answer: had finished

items.sentence: Leo said he ...... (can) meet us later.
items.answer: could

items.sentence: Mia said she ...... (have to) leave early.
items.answer: had to

items.sentence: They said they ...... (go) to the museum the next day.
items.answer: were going

sections.title: C: Time and place changes
sections.instructions: Rewrite with the correct time/place words.

items.sentence: 'I will call you tomorrow,' he said.
items.answer: He said he would call me the next day.

items.sentence: 'We met here yesterday,' they said.
items.answer: They said they had met there the day before.

items.sentence: 'I am busy now,' Anna said.
items.answer: Anna said she was busy then.

items.sentence: 'I saw him last week,' Ben said.
items.answer: Ben said he had seen him the week before.

items.sentence: 'I will stay here tonight,' she said.
items.answer: She said she would stay there that night.

items.sentence: 'I started this course two years ago,' Mark said.
items.answer: Mark said he had started that course two years before.

sections.title: D: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Nora said she **is / was** tired.
items.answer: was

items.sentence: They said they **can / could** help us.
items.answer: could

items.sentence: He said he **will / would** send the email later.
items.answer: would

items.sentence: Mum said we **have to / had to** tidy our room.
items.answer: had to

items.sentence: Paul said he had finished the task **yesterday / the day before**.
items.answer: the day before

items.sentence: She said she might visit us **next week / the following week**.
items.answer: the following week

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
"I will make you a scarf," my grandma said. **WOULD**
My grandma said she ...... me a scarf.
items.answer: would make

items.sentence:
"You have to visit the fashion show," she said. **HAD**
She said we ...... to visit the fashion show.
items.answer: had

items.sentence:
"I can swim very fast," Dan said. **COULD**
Dan said he ...... very fast.
items.answer: could swim

items.sentence:
"I'm drawing all day," Debbie said. **BEEN**
Debbie said she had ...... all day.
items.answer: been drawing

items.sentence:
"We are going to buy a new car," they said. **WERE**
They said they ...... going to buy a new car.
items.answer: were

items.sentence:
"I may visit my aunt tomorrow," Lucy said. **MIGHT**
Lucy said she ...... visit her aunt the next day.
items.answer: might

sections.title: F: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: He said he is very busy.
items.answer: He said he was very busy.

items.sentence: They said they will arrive at six.
items.answer: They said they would arrive at six.

items.sentence: Anna said she can help me.
items.answer: Anna said she could help me.

items.sentence: My dad said we have to leave early.
items.answer: My dad said we had to leave early.

items.sentence: She said she was there tomorrow.
items.answer: She said she would be there the next day.

items.sentence: Tom said he has finished his homework the day before.
items.answer: Tom said he had finished his homework the day before.
